title: oerstedella pseudowallisii
intro: Oerstedella pseudowallisii, also known as the Wallis' orchid, is a type of epiphytic orchid species native to New Caledonia. Research suggests that the plant has medicinal properties, particularly in its ability to inhibit the growth of certain bacteria and fungi, which may contribute to its potential use in treating infections. The health benefits of Oerstedella pseudowallisii are primarily based on its antimicrobial properties, which can help to reduce inflammation and promote wound healing. Herbal preparations of Oerstedella pseudowallisii are not well-documented, but extracts of the plant have been studied for their potential antimicrobial and antifungal properties.
